RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

At the Johnston (#274950) hearing the Court granted parties' request to also argue the same motions in the Lujan (#275496), Hanson (#275624), Liberty (#275871), Turner (#275910), and Harig (#276181) cases. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as to the Harig case: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 20, 2013 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 29, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) as well as the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(a). Trial is September 2, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is September 4, 2014. Last day for hearing summary motions is August 27, 2014. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P.
For any written contention discovery already served, the other side must respond within 20 days from this hearing or within the time dictate by the CCP, whichever is earlier. For any written contention discovery served on or after today, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email. It shall occur in person or via telephone.
Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than July 1, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 11, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on Aug. 27, 2014.
Fact discovery cut-off date is Aug. 15, 2014. Expert discovery cut off is Aug. 29, 2014.
No later than May 21, 2014, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than May 21, 2014, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.
Refer to the newly filed Trial Management Order for all deadlines for trial preparation.
Further case management conference on July 23, 2014 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on Sept. 2, 2014 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by Aug. 28, 2014 at 2:00pm.
The following deadlines are set: By 5/16 the VA Seattle employment record should be delivered to Berry & Berry. By 5/30 plaintiff will need to deliver the authorization to obtain the social security earning records for plaintiff's father, and the medical records to Berry & Berry. By 6/13 plaintiff's deposition should be completed.
Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
